hero_subtitle: SFTY Business supports compliance teams managing cross-border reporting by connecting regulatory rules, Travel Rule workflows, case evidence, and audit-ready reporting across jurisdictions.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Cross-border reporting slows due to fragmentation
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to manage jurisdiction-specific rules and Travel Rule policies across multiple regions with disconnected workflows and scattered evidence. This fragmentation creates delays, weak audit trails, and increases the complexity of preparing regulatory reports for different authorities.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes jurisdiction-specific Travel Rule policies, provider outputs, case management and evidence into one streamlined workflow to help VASP compliance teams prepare audit-ready cross-border regulatory reports efficiently.
workflow_title: From regulatory updates to report preparation
workflow_description: SFTY tracks regulatory changes across jurisdictions, maps them to internal rules, manages cases and evidence, and prepares multi-jurisdictional compliance reports efficiently.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented workflows. One evidence-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects provider outputs, regulatory rules, cases, and evidence into structured compliance workflows tailored for multi-jurisdictional reporting and cross-border operational control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing multi-jurisdictional reporting with structured workflows, audit-grade evidence preservation, banking due diligence, and enterprise deployment governance from day one.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how multi-jurisdictional reports link to cases and evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how provider outputs support Travel Rule compliance review
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how rules adapt to jurisdiction-specific Travel Rule policies
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for cross-border compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY supports multi-jurisdictional VASP compliance by connecting regulatory rules, Travel Rule policies, onboarding, case management and reporting workflows into one structured operational layer.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure reporting workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY organizes cross-border reporting by linking alerts, provider outputs, cases and regulatory triggers into reviewable workflows that align with jurisdiction-specific rules and reporting standards.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit evidence by maintaining case histories, decision logs and connected provider data to support traceable compliance records across cross-border workflows.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing provider stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers without disrupting current compliance technology stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a compliance control layer above providers, structuring their outputs into manageable workflows without replacing underlying systems.
final_title: Structure cross-border reporting workflows with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific Travel Rule policies, case evidence, and reporting for multi-jurisdictional VASP compliance.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
